The shares represent 0.0202% of voting rights and will be cancelled. Since December 2007, Rightmove has repurchased 565.6m shares. Following this transaction, 743.0m ordinary shares remain in issue, with 10.5m held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution maintaining capital allocation policy; demonstrates management confidence in valuation and provides modest EPS accretion; no material market implications.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pacific Horizon buys back 182,092 shares at 1,082.41p for treasury, reducing shares in issue to 78.0m.
summary: Pacific Horizon Investment Trust PLC purchased 182,092 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at 1,082.41p per share, to be held in treasury. The transaction increases treasury holdings to 14,046,142 shares, with 78,028,819 shares now in issue. This figure serves as the denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules calculations for shareholders.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks are typically neutral to mildly positive as they can support EPS but signal no urgent strategic catalyst.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: F&C Investment Trust PLC purchased 100,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities at prices between 338.60p and 338.80p, with a weighted average of 338.70p. The company now holds 363,049,480 treasury shares, leaving 1,884,226,584 shares in issue. Total voting rights stand at 1,884,226,584.
retail_take: Routine share buyback for treasury; typical capital management activity. No material implications for shareholders absent broader context on buyback rationale or scale.

headline: RESULTS OF ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING
brief: Workspace AGM: Board decisively re-elected; activist Saba's six nominees rejected; pre-emption rights disapplication fails.
summary: Workspace Group held its AGM on 23 July 2026. All 17 company-backed resolutions passed, with board re-elections supported by 87-100% of votes excluding Saba Capital's 27.86% holding. All six Saba requisitioned resolutions to appoint nominees failed, receiving only 1.5-13.3% support. Special resolution 18 to disapply pre-emption rights fell short of the required 75% threshold at 97.72%, leaving the company unable to proceed with planned equity flexibility.
retail_take: Board victory over activist investor is positive for management continuity, but failure of pre-emption waiver removes strategic flexibility for future capital raises, a modest negative offset by shareholder endorsement of existing strategy.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: JPMorgan US Smaller Co. IT repurchases 600,000 shares at 424p per share into Treasury.
summary: JPMorgan US Smaller Companies Investment Trust repurchased 600,000 ordinary shares at 424 pence per share on 23 July 2026. Following this transaction, the company holds 16,097,049 shares in Treasury, with 49,309,226 shares remaining in issue. The trust will only re-issue Treasury shares at a premium to net asset value.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and can support share price; this is a routine capital management action for investment trusts.

retail_take: Routine share buyback by investment trust; typical capital management action signalling confidence but with minimal direct impact on shareholders.

summary: Quilter Plc, via subsidiary Quilter Cheviot Limited, notified a reduction in voting rights in Ashoka WhiteOak Emerging Markets Trust Plc (ISIN GB00BMZR7D19) from 9.574% to 4.977%, crossing below the 5% disclosure threshold. The stake reduction occurred on 21 July 2026, notified to the issuer on 23 July 2026. The holding now comprises 2,063,509 direct voting shares with no financial instruments involved.
retail_take: Material reduction in a significant shareholder stake signals potential portfolio rebalancing by Quilter rather than fundamental concern; below-threshold position reduces reporting obligations going forward.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: Aberforth Smaller Companies Trust plc executed a market purchase of 30,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at 1,725.3643p per share, pursuant to authority granted at the March 2026 AGM. Following cancellation, 77,167,605 shares remain in issue. This represents part of a broader buyback programme under which 1,881,500 shares have been purchased and cancelled to date.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks by investment trusts typically support NAV per share and signal board confidence, though this is standard practice rather than a material market-moving event.

headline: Portfolio Update
brief: BlackRock Frontiers trust NAV declined 0.8% in June, outperforming benchmark; Kenya and Colombia drove gains.
summary: BlackRock Frontiers Investment Trust's NAV fell 0.8% in June 2026 (USD basis), outperforming its MSCI Frontier + Emerging ex Selected Countries benchmark which returned -1.2%. Kenya and Colombia delivered strongest returns (+10.1% and +12.2% respectively), with Kenyan banks KCB Group and Equity Group surging on robust Q1 earnings. Energy stocks dragged performance as oil prices fell; YPF, Perusahaan Gas Negara, and Yansab declined. The trust added positions in Georgian bank Lion Finance, Saudi insurance company Tawuniya, and Egyptian payments firm Fawry. Assets totalled £311.5m with a 4.0% net yield.
retail_take: Modest underperformance offset by benchmark outperformance suggests resilient stock-picking in volatile emerging/frontier markets; new positions indicate managers identifying opportunities amid attractive valuations.

headline: PayPoint plc : Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: PayPoint directors acquire shares via employee Share Incentive Plan on 22 July 2026 at £6.237 per share.
summary: Thirteen PDMRs including CEO Nicholas Wiles and CFO David Robert Harding participated in PayPoint's monthly Share Incentive Plan, each purchasing 20 Partnership Shares and receiving 20 Matching Shares on 22 July 2026 at £6.237 per share. Total transaction value per participant was £124.74. This represents routine participation in the company's employee share scheme across the management team.
retail_take: Routine disclosure with no material significance. Director participation in established SIP schemes is standard practice and indicates no exceptional confidence signals or concerns about valuation.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: BGEU purchases 200,000 own shares at 114.00p for treasury; total treasury holding now 114.1m shares.
summary: Baillie Gifford European Growth Trust plc (BGEU) purchased 200,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at 114.00p per share, to be held in treasury. retail_take: Routine share buyback for treasury; indicates management confidence at current valuation but represents standard capital management activity with no material implications.

headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: Fevara PLC discloses total voting rights of 51.79m ordinary shares in compliance with FCA rules.
summary: Fevara PLC has 51,793,549 issued ordinary shares of 2.5p each in issue, with no treasury holdings. Each share carries one vote at general meetings, establishing total voting rights of 51,793,549. This figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating notification thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure establishing the share count denominator for disclosure obligations; no material implications for share price or company fundamentals.

headline: TR-1: Notification of major holdings
brief: Morgan Stanley notifies 5.99% holding in Central Asia Metals PLC as of 21 July 2026.
summary: Morgan Stanley crossed a major holdings notification threshold on 21 July 2026, holding 5.988456% of voting rights in Central Asia Metals PLC (10.6m voting rights). The position comprises 5.638468% in direct shares (10.02m shares) and 0.349988% through financial instruments including securities lending recall rights (621,967 voting rights). Morgan Stanley notified the issuer on 23 July 2026. The previous notification showed a marginally higher position of 6.031484%, indicating a slight reduction in the stake.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of major holding threshold crossing. Slight stake reduction suggests no strategic intent signal; typical institutional portfolio management activity.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: SMT purchases 2m own shares at 1,309.95p for treasury on 23 July 2026.
summary: Scottish Mortgage Investment Trust PLC bought 2,000,000 ordinary shares at 1,309.95p per share on 23 July 2026, to be held in treasury. Following this transaction, the company now holds 410,558,993 shares in treasury, with 1,074,221,887 shares in issue. This share count is the relevant denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules notifications.
retail_take: Share buyback signals management confidence in valuation but is routine capital management; modest positive signal though not transformational given trust's size.

headline: Admission to Trading
brief: Fevara PLC (formerly Carr's Group) admits 4,465 ordinary shares to LSE Main Market trading.
summary: Fevara plc announced admission of 4,465 further ordinary shares (ISIN: GB00BRK01058) to trading on London Stock Exchange Main Market on 23 July 2026. The shares were issued on 3 June 2026 and admitted under an existing block admission facility. Total shares in issue following this admission now stand at 51,793,549. The shares are fully fungible with existing ordinary shares and no prospectus was required.
retail_take: Routine share issuance and admission notification under block facility mechanics; immaterial share count increase; no strategic read-through for investors.

headline: Issue of Equity
brief: Invesco Bond Income Plus issues 100,000 shares at 170.67p to meet secondary market demand.
summary: Invesco Bond Income Plus Limited has issued 100,000 ordinary shares at 170.67p per share under its block listing facility to satisfy secondary market demand. The allotment was authorised by shareholders and made under annual authority. Total shares in issue now stand at 287,434,911. New shares rank pari passu with existing shares.
retail_take: Routine equity issuance meeting market demand; indicates investor appetite for the fund but minimal direct impact on existing shareholders given modest share count increase of 0.035%.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: TR Property Investment Trust shareholders approve all AGM resolutions including share issuance and buyback authorities.
summary: All ordinary and special resolutions passed at TR Property's AGM on 23 July 2026. Directors granted authority to allot shares up to £26.2m (33% of issued capital) until October 2027. Special resolutions approved non-pre-emptive share issuance/treasury sales up to £7.9m (10% of capital) and market purchases of up to 47.6m shares (14.99% of issued capital). Full resolution text available in the company's Annual Report for year ended 31 March 2026.
retail_take: Routine shareholder approval for standard director powers and capital management flexibility. Share buyback authority provides downside support but no immediate strategic catalyst indicated.

headline: Oversubscribed UK Placing
brief: Scancell upsizes UK placing to £13m from £9m following strong oversubscription demand.
summary: Scancell Holdings (AIM: SCLP) raised £13.0m gross (c.$17.4m) via placing of 144.4m shares at undisclosed price, representing 13.9% of existing shares. The placing was significantly oversubscribed, prompting the company to increase from its initial £9.0m target to strengthen balance sheet and extend cash runway into Q3 2027. A concurrent retail offer of up to £2.3m closes 24 July, with admission to AIM expected 28 July. Panmure Liberum acted as sole placement agent.
retail_take: Strong investor demand enabling upsized fundraise signals market confidence in Scancell's strategy and cash position management, likely viewed positively despite dilution to existing shareholders.

headline: Share Purchase
brief: Martin Pike, incoming interim CEO, buys 50,000 shares at 58p, raising stake to 120,000 shares (0.09%).
summary: Martin Pike, Non-Executive Director and Incoming Interim Chief Executive Officer of Gateley (Holdings) PLC, purchased 50,000 ordinary shares on 23 July 2026 at 58 pence per share, investing £29,000. This transaction brings his total beneficial holding to 120,000 shares, representing 0.09% of issued share capital. The purchase was conducted on the London Stock Exchange.
retail_take: Modest insider buying ahead of interim CEO appointment signals confidence, though small stake size and low shareholding percentage limit inference; largely routine disclosure.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Lombard Odier Asset Management crosses 8.85% voting rights threshold in Dialight PLC.
summary: Lombard Odier Asset Management (Europe) Limited notified the FCA on 23 July 2026 that it crossed a major shareholding threshold in Dialight PLC on 22 July 2026. The entity now holds 3,576,531 voting rights, representing 8.85% of the company's total voting rights, all via direct share ownership with no financial instruments involved. The disclosure was made on behalf of accounts managed on a discretionary basis by the Lombard Odier Investment Managers group.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major shareholding crossing the 5% threshold; likely a non-event absent any indication of activist intent or strategic involvement from the fund manager.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Speedy Hire awards PSAs to CEO and CFO worth 75% of base salary each, vesting in 3 years subject to performance conditions.
summary: Dan Evans (CEO) received 1,999,340 PSAs and Judith Cottrell (CFO) received 1,530,491 PSAs on 23 July 2026 under the 2024 Performance Share Plan. Awards vest in three years subject to: 30% EPS targets (4-7p range for FY2029), 30% free cash flow targets (£20-32m), and 40% TSR performance versus FTSE SmallCap comparator group. Post-vesting holding period extends to year five. New remuneration policy pending shareholder approval at 2026 AGM will introduce restricted share awards alongside PSAs.
retail_take: Routine incentive award aligned with shareholder interests through multi-metric performance conditions; signals management confidence but standard practice for listed companies with no immediate value impact.

summary: Octopus AIM VCT 2 plc purchased and cancelled 675,533 ordinary shares of 0.01p each at 29.9651p per share on 23 July 2026. Following the transaction, total issued share capital and voting rights stand at 219,818,206 ordinary shares. This figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating disclosure notification thresholds under FCA rules.
retail_take: Share buyback and cancellation reduces share count, potentially accretive to NAV per share. Routine capital management activity; neutral to modestly positive signal on capital deployment.

headline: Purchase of Own Securities and Total Voting Rights

retail_take: Routine share buyback programme execution; modest capital reduction signals modest shareholder returns but no material strategic shift. Typical VCT management action.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Orchard Funding Group PLC confirms all AGM resolutions passed at 23 July 2026 meeting.
summary: Orchard Funding Group PLC, an AIM-listed finance company specialising in insurance premium finance and professional fee funding, held its Annual General Meeting on 23 July 2026. All resolutions put before shareholders were duly passed. Detailed voting results have been made available on the company's website. The company operates through wholly-owned subsidiaries Bexhill UK Limited and Orchard Funding Limited, serving insurance brokers and professional firms across the UK.
retail_take: Routine governance disclosure; all resolutions passing is standard practice and carries no material implications for shareholders or near-term trading direction.

headline: Result of Annual General Meeting
brief: Octopus AIM VCT plc reports all 16 resolutions passed at 23 July 2026 AGM with strong shareholder support.
summary: All resolutions carried on a show of hands at the Annual General Meeting held 23 July 2026. Support levels ranged from 87.66% to 93.43% of votes cast across the 16 resolutions. Only 1.48% of the company's 248.9 million issued shares lodged valid proxies for the meeting. Resolutions covered routine governance matters including director appointments and re-appointments.
retail_take: Routine AGM approval with robust shareholder backing across all proposals; standard governance matter with no contentious issues or unusual voting patterns identified.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: XPS Pensions execs exercise PSP options and sell shares to cover tax; CFO transfers 50k shares to spouse.
summary: On 22 July 2026, Co-CEOs Paul Cuff and Ben Bramhall and CFO Snehal Shah exercised nominal-cost PSP options granted July 2023, each acquiring 333,200 and 241,239 shares respectively at 0.05p. Both co-CEOs sold 157,182 shares at 329.9p and Shah sold 113,801 shares at 329.9p to cover income tax and National Insurance liabilities. Shah additionally transferred 50,000 shares to spouse Meeta Shah for nil consideration.
retail_take: Routine director transaction disclosure showing option vesting and tax-driven share sales at 329.9p; no material implications for shareholders or market sentiment.

headline: Result of AGM - Update Statement
brief: Fevara addresses shareholder dissent over non-exec director's board meeting absences at February 2026 AGM.
summary: At Fevara plc's 11 February 2026 AGM, all resolutions passed, but Non-Executive Director Gillian Watson's re-election received 39.26% votes against. Post-AGM shareholder engagement attributed dissent to Watson missing two late-scheduled board meetings during the financial year. The Board maintains Watson demonstrates continued commitment and effectiveness, stating absences don't raise concerns regarding her overall contribution. Fevara, a livestock supplements specialist headquartered in Carlisle with five manufacturing sites globally, reported FY25 revenue of £78.8m and adjusted operating profit of £3.7m.
retail_take: Non-event governance disclosure; modest shareholder dissent (39.26%) on director attendance is below concerning levels, and board's reassurance regarding Watson's contribution likely sufficient to avoid material market impact.

headline: Issue of Equity

summary: BlackRock American Income Trust plc sold 100,000 ordinary shares from treasury at 277.30 pence per share, representing a premium to net asset value. Treasury holdings reduced to 33,601,167 shares, with 61,760,138 shares now in issue. The voting rights figure of 61,760,138 takes effect 24 July 2026 for disclosure calculation purposes.
retail_take: Routine treasury share issuance at a premium to NAV is marginally constructive but largely a housekeeping disclosure with minimal market impact.

The stake is held entirely through indirect voting rights attached to shares. The notification was filed on 22 July 2026. No financial instruments are involved.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholder stake reduction below the 14% level; standard regulatory notification with no material implications for market sentiment or company strategy.

headline: Cancellation of Treasury Shares

summary: Lion Finance Group PLC has cancelled 29,399 treasury shares on 23 July 2026, completing its GEL 55 million buyback programme announced in May 2026. This was the final tranche of a broader buyback programme launched in August 2025 comprising four phases (GEL 98.0m, GEL 51.5m, GEL 53.5m, and GEL 55m). Total shares cancelled across the entire programme reached 777,541. Post-cancellation, the company holds zero treasury shares with 43,086,035 voting rights in issue.
retail_take: Routine completion of previously announced capital return programme. Share cancellations reduce outstanding share count, supporting EPS accretion. Non-event for market as programme was pre-announced and expected.

The pension partnership now holds 1,567,913 voting rights representing 4.93% of the company's shares, with no additional voting rights via financial instruments. The notification was filed on the same day the threshold was crossed.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a significant pension fund stake crossing regulatory thresholds; indicates institutional confidence but no material strategic implications for retail investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Kelso Group Holdings repurchased 1.75m shares at 3p each for treasury.
summary: Kelso Group Holdings PLC purchased 1,746,302 ordinary shares between 15-20 July 2026 at a consistent price of 3.0 pence per share, holding them in treasury. The share buyback reduces total voting rights to 460,542,034 while maintaining issued share capital at 464,069,995. Treasury holdings now total 3,527,961 shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback at consistent pricing; typical capital management for an investment vehicle. No material signal regarding investment strategy or financial stress.

headline: Dividend Declaration
brief: Gresham House Renewable Energy VCT2 declares 17.0p dividend per Ordinary Share following solar asset sale completion.
summary: Gresham House Renewable Energy VCT 2 PLC has declared a 17.0p dividend per Ordinary Share and 0.0p per 'A' Share, payable 21 August 2026 to shareholders registered by 31 July 2026, ex-dividend 30 July. The dividend follows completion of the sale of the Company's remaining solar assets announced 23 June 2026. The Company plans to enter members' voluntary liquidation in September 2026, with further distributions anticipated by end H1 2027. Dividend payment is subject to unaudited accounts validating distributable reserves.
retail_take: Substantial capital return from asset sale signals orderly wind-down; liquidation timing and further distributions dependent on liquidator discretion, creating execution risk for remaining investors.

headline: Dividend Declaration
brief: Gresham House Renewable Energy VCT1 declares 17.0p dividend per Ordinary Share following solar asset sale completion.
summary: The Board has declared a dividend of 17.0p per Ordinary Share and 0.0p per 'A' Share, payable 21 August 2026 to shareholders on the register at 31 July 2026 (ex-dividend 30 July). This follows the June 2026 completion of the company's remaining solar asset sale. The company anticipates entering members' voluntary liquidation in September 2026, with a small capital distribution expected by end of H1 2027, with further distributions at the liquidator's discretion.
retail_take: Material capital return to shareholders as VCT winds down; liquidation process underway with additional distributions anticipated, suggesting orderly exit and value realisation for investors.

headline: Settlement of NEBA deferred consideration
brief: Team PLC settles Neba acquisition deferred consideration via 1.93m share issuance at 15p per share.
summary: Team PLC is issuing 1,929,627 ordinary shares at 15 pence per share to settle final deferred consideration for the Neba Financial Solutions acquisition completed in December 2023. Director John Beverley, the seller, will receive the shares, with admission to AIM expected on 28 July 2026. Post-admission, the company will have 123,566,709 shares in issue.
retail_take: Director's willingness to accept shares as settlement signals confidence in the business; the 15p reference price matches AIM trading levels, suggesting no material valuation concern.

headline: Appointment of Non-Executive Director
brief: Team PLC appoints John Cusins as independent Non-Executive Director, effective immediately.
summary: John Cusins, aged 54, joins Team PLC's board as an independent Non-Executive Director with immediate effect, following announcement on 30 June 2026. Cusins co-founded Lumin Wealth Management, scaling it from £12m to £1.4bn AUM before overseeing majority stake sale to VZ Group. He holds senior experience at UBS, Dresdner Bank and KPMG, with expertise in wealth management, private equity and capital markets. Cusins owns 1,375,750 TEAM shares directly plus 61,162 via investment company.
retail_take: Positive signal: experienced wealth management executive with proven scaling track record and significant personal shareholding aligns interests as company pursues growth and international expansion.

headline: Litigation
brief: Meridian Hospital Company faces dispute with NHS Trust over Concession Agreement deductions; formal papers expected soon.
summary: Lewisham and Greenwich NHS Trust issued a dispute notice on 25 February 2026 regarding interpretation of deductions under the Concession Agreement with Meridian Hospital Company PLC (issuer of £91.2m 4.375% bonds due 2028). Discussions ended 11 July; formal dispute papers are imminent. The parties are appointing a dispute panel. The company states this does not constitute a Trigger Event under the Bond Trust Deed and will not materially impact debt servicing; no Event of Default has been triggered.
retail_take: Dispute is material but company asserts no immediate covenant breach or payment risk; however, unresolved NHS contract friction could create longer-term uncertainty for bondholders and cash flow trajectory.

headline: Result of General Meeting
brief: Smiths Group shareholders overwhelmingly approve share buyback programme at General Meeting.
summary: Smiths Group plc held a General Meeting on 23 July 2026 to approve a share buyback resolution. The resolution passed as a special resolution with 97.42% voting in favour (225.1m votes) against 2.58% opposition (5.96m votes). Turnout was 77.82% of the 296.95m issued shares. The company holds no treasury shares and has one vote per share.
retail_take: Strong shareholder approval signals confidence in capital allocation strategy and cash generation. Share buybacks typically support EPS and share price, viewed positively by markets absent other concerns.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Morgan Stanley's holding in Midwich Group PLC rises to 6.19% of voting rights, crossing notification threshold.
summary: Morgan Stanley Capital Services LLC increased its voting rights holding in Midwich Group PLC to 6,503,943 shares (6.185682%) from 5.997099%, crossing the 6% disclosure threshold on 16 July 2026. The notification was filed on 20 July 2026. The holding was built through two controlled chains under Morgan Stanley's ultimate control, with no financial instruments involved.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholding change; Morgan Stanley's incremental stake increase signals investor confidence but lacks material news implications for retail investors.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Motorpoint shareholders approve all 18 AGM resolutions with strong majorities at 23 July 2026 meeting.
summary: Motorpoint Group held its AGM on 23 July 2026 with 72.4% proxy voting participation (60.5m shares). All 18 resolutions passed overwhelmingly, including adoption of FY2026 accounts, director re-elections, and remuneration approvals. Resolutions 1, 2, and 5 achieved 100% approval. Three resolutions on share allotment authorities (14-16) received notably lower support at 88.64%, though still approved. All board members retained their positions with 99.95-99.99% approval.
retail_take: Routine governance approval with strong shareholder backing. Minor concern that share allotment resolutions received ~11% opposition, but this is standard for discretionary authorities and unlikely to materially concern markets.

headline: Posting of Annual Report and Notice of AGM
brief: Cavendish PLC posts 2026 Annual Report; AGM scheduled for 18 August 2026 in London.
summary: Cavendish plc (AIM: CAV) has published its Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 March 2026 on its website and will mail copies to shareholders. The Annual General Meeting will be held on Tuesday, 18 August 2026 at 1:30pm at the company's registered office, 1 Bartholomew Close, London EC1A 7BL. Co-CEOs Julian Morse and John Farrugia, along with CFO Ben Procter, lead the investment banking and corporate finance advisory firm.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of annual results and governance documentation; no material operational or financial data disclosed in this announcement to drive market reaction.

headline: Completion of the sale of Infinity House
brief: Character Group completes £9.8m sale of Infinity House warehouse in Lancashire to Robertsbridge Property.
summary: Character Group PLC has completed the sale of its Infinity House warehouse and office complex in Middleton, Lancashire for £9.8 million gross proceeds. The sale followed exercise of a purchase option by Robertsbridge Property Co Limited on 25 June 2026, with completion achieved 23 July 2026. The funds have been received and will be applied to the company's general cash resources, strengthening its balance sheet.
retail_take: Asset sale generating substantial cash inflow likely viewed positively as balance sheet strengthening for the AIM-listed toy distributor, though timing and use of proceeds will be key to investor assessment.

headline: Change of Registered Office
brief: Cornish Metals plc changes registered office to Truro, Cornwall.
summary: Cornish Metals has relocated its registered office to Falcon House, Charles Street, Truro, TR1 2PH. The company is advancing the South Crofty tin mine project in Cornwall, with planning permission valid until 2071. The mine is expected to produce approximately 4,700 tonnes of tin-in-concentrate annually, positioning it as potentially Europe or North America's first primary tin producer, supporting domestic supply and creating over 300 jobs.
retail_take: Administrative relocation reflecting company's operational focus on South Crofty tin development. Positioning closer to asset is operationally sensible but represents a routine, non-material housekeeping change.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Societe Generale increases JTC PLC stake to 6.02% via share acquisition and CFDs.
summary: Societe Generale crossed a notifiable threshold in JTC PLC on 22 July 2026, increasing its holding from 5.73% to 6.02% of voting rights. The position comprises 10.28m shares (5.89%) and 234k voting rights via cash-settled contracts for difference expiring May 2028 (0.13%). The notification was filed 23 July 2026. This represents an acquisition of voting rights by the London-registered bank.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major shareholder threshold crossing; Societe Generale's modest stake increase shows continued confidence but carries no material strategic implications for broader market sentiment.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Experian CEO Brian Cassin granted 50,684 performance shares under PSP on 23 July 2026.
summary: Experian plc notified the market of a Performance Share Plan award to Brian Cassin, Chief Executive Officer, on 23 July 2026. The award grants a conditional right to receive a maximum of 50,684 ordinary shares (10 US cents each) for nil consideration, subject to performance conditions being met. The reference price used for the award was 2,728.6667 pence per share.
retail_take: Routine executive remuneration disclosure under MAR regulations; demonstrates standard long-term incentive alignment but no material implications for broader shareholder base.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: JPMorgan Chase crosses 9.71% voting threshold in JTC PLC via direct shares and equity swaps.
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co., acting through J.P. Morgan Securities PLC, notified on 23 July 2026 of crossing a major holdings threshold in JTC PLC on 21 July. The position comprises 6.86% voting rights from 11.99m direct shares and 2.85% from cash-settled equity swaps across multiple expiry dates (2026-2032), totalling 9.71% of voting rights. This represents an increase from the previous 8.88% notification, driven primarily by higher direct share holdings (up from 6.03%).
retail_take: Routine disclosure reflecting a passive increase in JPMorgan's existing stake; no material strategic implications signalled. Market likely treats as non-event unless followed by activist intentions.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Boston Partners reduces Hikma stake to 5.67% from 7.05% following share disposal.
summary: Boston Partners (formerly Robeco Investment Management) crossed a notification threshold on 21 July 2026 by reducing its voting rights in Hikma Pharmaceuticals from 7.052% to 5.670%. The stake comprises 11.84m shares (5.61% direct holdings) plus financial instruments totaling 0.06%. The reduction was notified to the FCA on 22 July 2026. ORIX Corporation is the ultimate controlling entity.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholding decrease below the 5% notifiable threshold; signals portfolio rebalancing rather than fundamental concerns about Hikma's prospects.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Octopus Investments crosses 18% voting threshold in dotDigital Group plc on 22 July 2026.
summary: Octopus Investments Limited, controlled by Octopus Capital Limited, notified the market on 23 July 2026 that its voting rights in dotDigital Group plc increased to 18.060% from 17.060% on 22 July 2026. The stake comprises 54.77 million voting rights attached to shares, held indirectly with no financial instruments involved. This represents a 1% increase crossing a regulatory notification threshold.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of major shareholding notification. Modest 1% increase suggests continued shareholder confidence but no material strategic signal; market likely treats as administrative disclosure.

headline: Results of AGM
brief: Kistos Holdings plc shareholders approved all 13 AGM resolutions with overwhelming majorities on 23 July 2026.
summary: All resolutions passed at Kistos Holdings' AGM on 23 July 2026, including ordinary resolutions on annual accounts, remuneration, board re-elections (Austin, Benmore, Booth, Mann, Thomson, Pawson), and BDO LLP's reappointment as auditor. Special resolutions passed for director share allotment authority (99.95%), share buyback authority (98.73%), and new articles of association (99.99%). Pre-emption rights disapplication received 94.29% support.
retail_take: Routine governance disclosure with strong shareholder backing across all matters. No material concerns signalled; standard annual approval processes completed successfully.

headline: Annual Financial Report
brief: Southern Gas Networks releases FY2026 annual report covering year ended 31 March 2026 with RIIO-GD3 regulatory period commencing.
summary: SGN's annual report details operations of regulated gas distribution network serving 4.0 million domestic customers and 131,000+ commercial customers across south and southeast England. Company operates under RIIO-GD2 framework through March 2026, transitioning to RIIO-GD3 (April 2026-March 2031). Ofgem's December 2025 final determination imposed 15% reduction in allowances versus business plan, prompting CMA appeal on three grounds. Strategic priorities focus on safety, customer service, people, productivity and decarbonisation including biomethane and hydrogen initiatives.
retail_take: Routine annual disclosure with regulatory context; the material issue is the 15% allowance cut and CMA appeal outcome, not yet resolved, creating near-term uncertainty for financial projections and debt investor sentiment.

headline: Results of Annual General Meeting
brief: Vp plc's AGM approves all resolutions including final dividend of 28.0p per share and board appointments.
summary: All 14 resolutions passed at Vp plc's 23 July 2026 AGM with overwhelming majorities (>91%). Shareholders approved the final dividend of 28.0 pence per ordinary share, re-elected five directors, and elected new CEO Alice Woodwark. Mark Bottomley's re-election as independent director received notably lower support at 91.5% versus 99%+ for others, though independent shareholders approved it at 76.2%. PwC reappointed as auditor. 78.24% of issued share capital voted.
retail_take: Routine AGM approval with strong shareholder backing across all resolutions. Mark Bottomley's weaker independent shareholder support (76.2%) warrants monitoring but poses no immediate governance concern. Non-event for market.

headline: Annual Financial Report
brief: Scotland Gas Networks publishes FY2026 results; faces challenging RIIO-GD3 regulatory settlement with 15% allowance reduction.
summary: Scotland Gas Networks PLC released audited financial statements for year ended 31 March 2026. The company operates Scotland's gas distribution network serving 1.8 million homes and 58,000+ commercial customers across 25,000km of pipeline. Ofgem's December 2025 RIIO-GD3 final determination imposed a 15% reduction in allowances versus business plan request, prompting three-ground CMA appeal. Company maintains 99.99% network reliability and emphasizes safety, customer service, and decarbonization through biomethane and hydrogen initiatives.
retail_take: Regulatory headwind from 15% allowance cut and pending CMA appeal creates uncertainty; appeal outcome determines medium-term financial viability and investment capacity for this regulated utility.

headline: Result of General Meeting
brief: Maintel Holdings shareholders approved all five resolutions at General Meeting on 23 July 2026.
summary: All resolutions passed as ordinary resolutions at Maintel's General Meeting held 23 July 2026. Annual Report and Accounts received with 100% approval (8.56m votes for). Directors' Remuneration Report approved 96.85% (8.29m for). Remuneration Policy passed 74.80% (8.44m for). RSM UK Audit LLP re-appointed with 99.78% support (8.54m for). Audit fee authorisation approved unanimously. Total voting rights: 18.73m ordinary shares outstanding.
retail_take: Routine shareholder approval of standard corporate governance matters. Strong backing for remuneration policy despite 25.2% opposition suggests some investor concern but no material dissent.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Arkle Resources passes all AGM resolutions; uranium-focused explorer reports maiden Namibia drilling underway with visible mineralisation logged.
summary: All shareholder resolutions passed at the 23 July 2026 AGM. The company, repositioned as uranium-focused energy metals explorer following January 2026 acquisition of Namibia Uranium Pty Ltd (540 km² in Erongo belt), has commenced fully-funded maiden RC drilling on EPL 8995 with 66 holes completed for 1,615m. Carnotite visually logged in three holes; first assays expected early September. Separately, step-out drilling at Stonepark zinc project (21.38% interest, operator Group Eleven) intersected 2.8m at 8.3% Zn+Pb. Botswana lithium brine programme advancing with EIA targeted 2026.
retail_take: Positive read: visible uranium mineralisation in first holes, maiden assays imminent, fully funded through H2 2026, and material zinc discovery at Stonepark demonstrate operational momentum across three-commodity portfolio in tier-1 jurisdictions.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Merrill Lynch International's holding in Edinburgh Worldwide Investment Trust falls below 5% threshold.
summary: Bank of America Corporation, via Merrill Lynch International, crossed a notification threshold on 22 July 2026, with voting rights decreasing from 5.067% to 5.012%. The position comprises 17,282,996 direct voting rights (4.997%), 1,079 rights to recall (0.0003%), and 50,880 swap-based voting rights (0.0147%). The disclosure was notified to the regulator on 23 July 2026 in London.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major holding adjustment below the 5% threshold; typical institutional trading activity with no strategic implications for the fund.

headline: Completion of the acquisition of Cormetech
brief: Johnson Matthey completes Cormetech acquisition, reshaping portfolio around emissions control and platinum group metals.
summary: Johnson Matthey PLC completed the acquisition of Cormetech Inc. on 23 July 2026, announced on 28 May 2026. Combined with the sale of Catalyst Technologies (completed 17 July 2026), this reshapes JM's portfolio into a focused business centred on platinum group metal chemistry and emissions control. The Cormetech combination with JM's Clean Air Solutions creates a global leader in stationary emissions control, particularly in data centre markets, positioning the group for sustainable cash generation.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively as portfolio transformation creating a leaner, focused business in attractive growth markets with strong cash generation potential, offsetting prior asset sales.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: 1607 Capital Partners reduces stake in Finsbury Growth & Income Trust to 4.94% from 5.00%.
summary: 1607 Capital Partners, LLC (Richmond, VA) crossed the notifiable threshold on 22 July 2026, reducing its voting rights in Finsbury Growth & Income Trust PLC (ISIN GB0007816068) from 5.002% to 4.936%, representing 4,972,553 voting rights. The disclosure was made on 23 July 2026. State Street Global Advisors, Northern Trust Company, and Bank of New York Mellon hold indirect voting interests through the notifying party.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a minor stake reduction below 5% threshold; no material implications for investors. Passive institutional shareholding adjustment.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: easyJet executives granted SAYE options: Chief Data Officer 2,275 shares, Chief People Officer 3,185 shares at £4.00 exercise price.
summary: On 15 July 2026, two PDMRs at easyJet received Save As You Earn scheme options: Opal Perry (Chief Data and Technology Officer) granted 2,275 options and Elly Tomlins (Chief People Officer) granted 3,185 options, both at £4.00 exercise price with expected maturity 1 September 2029. The SAYE is an all-employee three-year savings scheme; options become exercisable for six months post-maturity subject to continued employment.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of all-employee share scheme grants to senior staff; standard executive retention mechanism with no performance conditions, indicating ordinary compensation practice rather than material corporate event.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: M&G Plc crosses 5% voting rights threshold in Oxford Biomedica, holding 6.26m shares.
summary: M&G Plc notified on 23 July 2026 that it crossed the 5% voting rights threshold in Oxford Biomedica PLC on 22 July 2026. M&G holds 6,065,436 direct voting rights (5.01%) plus 192,100 lent shares with recall rights (0.16%), totalling 5.17% of voting rights. The holding increased from the previous notification level of 5.09%, representing a modest accumulation by the major asset manager.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholder crossing regulatory thresholds; M&G's incremental position increase suggests ongoing investment conviction but poses no material strategic implications.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Aberforth Partners' stake in Headlam Group falls to 4.14% from 12.93% following share disposal.
summary: Aberforth Partners LLP, through nominee Nortrust Nominees Ltd, reduced its voting rights in Headlam Group PLC from 12.926% to 4.140% (3.35m shares) on 21 July 2026. The threshold crossing was notified to the issuer on 23 July. The disposal represents a significant reduction in the fund manager's major holding, moving below the 5% disclosure threshold level.
retail_take: Substantial stake reduction by major shareholder suggests either portfolio rebalancing or loss of confidence; market likely to scrutinise reasons for the 65% reduction in holding.

headline: Grant of Options and Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Jet2 executives granted share options and deferred awards totalling 205k+ shares on 22 July 2026.
summary: Steve Heapy (CEO) received options over 102,242 shares under the Share Reward Plan and 15,336 shares via the Senior Executive Incentive Deferred Award Plan. Gary Brown (Group CFO) was granted options over 78,061 shares under SRP and 11,709 shares under SEIP. All grants made on 22 July 2026 at nominal par value of £0.0125 per share, disclosed in accordance with UK Market Abuse Regulation requirements.
retail_take: Routine executive incentive grant reflecting competitive compensation practices; indicates management confidence but represents standard share-based remuneration with no material market implications.

headline: Notification about GDRs Purchase by PDMR of MHP
brief: MHP SE deputy CEO purchases 6,097 GDRs worth ~$50k on LSE
summary: Serhii Dobrohorskyi, Deputy Chief Executive Officer for Agribusiness at MHP SE, purchased 6,097 Global Depositary Receipts at $8.20 per share on 20-21 July 2026 for $49,995.40 total. The transaction was executed via Interactive Brokers Group and disclosed under UK Market Abuse Regulation requirements as a PDMR insider transaction notification.
retail_take: Routine insider buy signal with modest scale; modest investment by senior executive suggests some confidence but insufficient size to move market sentiment materially.

headline: First Abu Dhabi Bank Sukuk US$5bn Third Supplement
brief: FAB issues third supplement to US$5bn sukuk prospectus, updating interim financials and regional conflict disclosures.
summary: First Abu Dhabi Bank's sukuk trustee issued a third supplement dated 23 July 2026 to its base prospectus for the US$5bn trust certificate issuance programme. The supplement incorporates unaudited H1 2026 interim financial statements and significantly updates risk factors, particularly a detailed expansion of regional conflict risks following Israel-US-Iran hostilities in February-April 2026, including airspace closures, shipping disruptions across the Strait of Hormuz, and potential impacts on asset quality and funding costs. A material change statement covers December 2025 to June 2026.
retail_take: Routine regulatory document updating investor disclosures with latest financials and geopolitical risks. The expanded conflict narrative reflects transparent risk communication rather than new operational impairment; no market-moving news.

headline: First Abu Dhabi Bank EMTN US$30bn First Supplement
brief: FAB publishes first supplement to €30bn EMTN programme base prospectus, incorporating H1 2026 financials and updating geopolitical risk disclosures.
summary: First Abu Dhabi Bank filed a first supplement dated 23 July 2026 to its US$30bn Euro Medium Term Note Programme base prospectus. The supplement incorporates unaudited condensed interim financial statements for the six months ended 30 June 2026 and updates risk factors regarding regional conflict between Israel, the US and Iran, including recent February 2026 escalation, Iranian retaliatory strikes on GCC states including the UAE, Strait of Hormuz disruption, and airspace closures. The material change statement confirms no significant change in the Bank's financial position since 30 June 2026.
retail_take: Routine prospectus maintenance filing with updated geopolitical risk disclosure reflecting recent regional tensions affecting UAE operations and shipping; standard administrative disclosure with no implications for existing investors.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Inspiration Healthcare Group PLC confirms all AGM resolutions passed at 23 July 2026 meeting.
summary: Inspiration Healthcare Group plc (AIM: IHC), a neonatal intensive care medical device company, announced that all resolutions proposed at its Annual General Meeting on 23 July 2026 were duly passed. The company, which designs and manufactures specialist medical technology for neonatal care, operates manufacturing facilities in Croydon, South London and Melbourne, Florida, with distribution across 75+ countries. Voting results will be published on the company's website.
retail_take: Routine governance disclosure indicating successful shareholder approval of AGM resolutions; no material business updates or strategic announcements; non-event for markets.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Ninety One directors acquire shares via Marathon Trust vehicle on 22 July 2026.
summary: Forty Two Point Two, a legal entity wholly owned by the Marathon Trust and benefiting directors including Hendrik du Toit and Kim McFarland, acquired 299,749 ordinary shares in Ninety One plc on 22 July 2026. Purchases occurred at three price points: 26,749 shares at GBP 2.0848, 60,720 shares at GBP 2.0809, and 212,531 shares at GBP 2.0968. Total investment approximately GBP 624,000. Directors obtained clearance for these transactions under UK MAR and JSE requirements.
retail_take: Director-aligned trust buying shares signals management confidence in company value, though size and structure suggest routine portfolio management rather than material conviction signal.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Halma PLC's 2026 AGM passes all 21 resolutions with overwhelming shareholder support across board re-elections and capital authorities.
summary: All resolutions passed at Halma's 23 July 2026 AGM with strong backing. Ordinary resolutions (1-17) received 92.79-100% support; special resolutions (18-21) achieved 93-99.66% approval. Key outcomes include annual accounts and final dividend approval, re-election of all 11 board members (96.50-99.44% support), auditor reappointment (99.87%), and authorities for share allotment and buybacks. Shareholder participation reached 77.11% of voting shares.
retail_take: Routine disclosure demonstrating standard governance compliance with no contentious votes; strong approval margins indicate shareholder confidence in management and board, presenting a non-event to markets.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Oxford Instruments AGM: all 21 resolutions passed with strong shareholder support on 23 July 2026.
summary: Oxford Instruments held its 2026 AGM on 23 July with all 21 resolutions approved by shareholders. Annual Report adoption passed 99.99%, Directors' Remuneration Policy 98.34%, and dividend declaration 99.99%. All six director re-elections passed, though Neil Carson received 9.41% votes against. Deloitte LLP retained as auditor (99.99% approval). Share allotment and buyback authorities approved. 81.11% of issued share capital voted.
retail_take: Routine governance disclosure; overwhelming shareholder support across all resolutions indicates no material governance concerns or investor dissent; non-event for markets.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Finance Director Steve Mant undertakes Bed & ISA transfer; beneficial holding unchanged.
summary: Steve Mant, Finance Director of Castings PLC, completed a Bed & ISA transfer on 22-23 July 2026 involving 6,006 shares. He sold shares at 325.06p and repurchased 6,006 shares at an aggregated price of 335.38p, with an additional 47 shares purchased on 23 July at 334.20p to restore his total holding to pre-transaction levels. The transaction was administrative in nature and did not alter his beneficial ownership position.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of administrative share transfer for tax-wrapper purposes; no material significance to shareholding or company prospects.

headline: TR1 Notification of major interest in shares
brief: Artemis Investment Management LLP's SSP Group stake falls to 6.83% from 7.30% following share disposal on 22 July.
summary: Artemis Investment Management LLP notified the FCA on 23 July 2026 that its voting rights in SSP Group PLC decreased to 6.83126% (52.09m shares) from 7.295295% previously, following an acquisition or disposal of voting rights on 22 July 2026. The stake is held directly through equity shares with ISIN GB00BGBN7C04. Artemis Fund Managers Limited holds 5.02175% of voting rights within the same structure. No financial instruments are involved.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a shareholding reduction; Artemis remains a substantial holder but below the 7% threshold, which may indicate portfolio rebalancing rather than strategic concern about the company.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Francoise Phillips acquires 1.84m shares in Northamber PLC, reaching 6.79% voting rights threshold.
summary: Francoise Phillips crossed the 5% notifiable threshold in Northamber PLC on 22 July 2026, acquiring 1,840,000 shares representing 6.79% of voting rights. The acquisition was notified to the issuer and FCA on 23 July 2026. Phillips holds shares directly with no financial instruments involved. Total voting rights in the company stand at 27.1 million.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding acquisition; signals potential new significant stakeholder but no immediate strategic implications disclosed. Market likely views as neutral unless Phillips declares intentions.

headline: Publication of a Supplementary Prospectus
brief: National Gas Transmission publishes supplementary prospectus incorporating FY2026 results for €10bn EMTN programme.
summary: National Gas Transmission PLC published a supplementary prospectus on 23 July 2026, incorporating audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 March 2026. The supplement updates the base prospectus dated 16 December 2025 for the €10 billion Euro Medium Term Note Programme. The issuer confirms no significant change in financial performance/position or material adverse change in prospects since 31 March 2026, with full year results published 20 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine regulatory filing updating debt programme prospectus with latest audited financials; procedural disclosure required for ongoing EMTN issuance facility with no material news content.

headline: Intimation of 42nd Annual General Meeting
brief: GAIL (India) Limited schedules 42nd AGM for 27 August 2026 via video conference; record date set for 2 September for final dividend.
summary: GAIL (India) Limited has announced its 42nd Annual General Meeting will be held on Thursday, 27 August 2026 at 11:30 a.m. via video conferencing. The company has set Wednesday, 2 September 2026 as the record date for determining shareholder entitlement to the final dividend for FY 2025-26, contingent on member approval at the AGM. Annual report and AGM notice will be distributed electronically to members with registered email addresses.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of standard corporate governance proceedings; no material implications for investors beyond confirmation of dividend eligibility processes and shareholder meeting logistics.

headline: Result of AGM and General Meeting
brief: Beowulf Mining shareholders overwhelmingly approve AGM and GM resolutions including capital reorganisation and fundraising waiver.
summary: All resolutions passed with strong majorities (97-99%). AGM approved annual accounts, director re-elections, and auditor reappointment. GM approved Takeover Panel waiver, capital reorganisation subdividing 64.7m shares into 0.1p ordinary shares and 4.9p deferred shares, and share allotment authorities. Trading in new ordinary shares expected to commence 24 July 2026. Subscription conditional on Swedish FDI approval, anticipated by 13 August 2026, with financing closing 2-3 days thereafter.
retail_take: Smooth shareholder approval removes key execution risks for the fundraising and restructuring. FDI approval timing now critical path; positive if Sweden signs off promptly, neutral pending approval outcome.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Societe Generale increases holding in Senior PLC to 11.06% of voting rights, crossing notification threshold.
summary: Societe Generale notified Senior PLC on 23 July 2026 of a major holding increase, with voting rights rising from 10.25% to 11.06% as of 22 July 2026. The position comprises 46,386,014 direct voting rights (11.06%) plus 3 voting rights via a cash-settled contract for difference expiring 25 November 2026. The threshold crossing triggered the TR-1 notification requirement under UK disclosure rules.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding change. Modest 0.81pp increase signals continued investor interest but lacks material strategic implications for the engineering company.

headline: Results of General Meeting
brief: NCC Group shareholders overwhelmingly approve share premium cancellation and buyback authorisations at GM.
summary: At its 23 July 2026 General Meeting, NCC Group PLC shareholders passed all three special resolutions with over 99% support. Resolutions approved: cancellation of £225m Share Premium Account credit (99.98% for), authorisation for market purchases via Tender Offer (99.76% for), and authorisation for new Buyback Programme (99.98% for). Voter turnout was 62.18% of issued share capital (284.5m ordinary shares).
retail_take: Routine shareholder approval for capital management and buyback initiatives. Strong voting margins indicate shareholder consensus; market likely sees this as non-event housekeeping with no material strategic implications.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: Athelney Trust NAV fell 3.8% to 163.1p in H1 2026 amid challenging UK smaller-cap markets; interim dividend maintained at 2.4p.
summary: Athelney Trust PLC reported NAV decline to 163.1p (from 169.5p at year-end 2025) as UK Smaller Companies sector faced geopolitical uncertainty and investor flight to larger stocks. Share price held steady at 165p, narrowing discount to NAV to 1.1% versus 12.46% sector average. Revenue fell 24.5% to £101,002; interim dividend of 2.4p declared for September 2026 payment. NAV total return was 0.71% including dividends. Portfolio companies including Wise, AJ Bell, and Cake Box showed resilient operational performance despite market volatility.
retail_take: Weak near-term performance offset by attractive valuations, narrow discount to NAV, and strong underlying company fundamentals; likely read as cautious-to-neutral pending sentiment recovery in UK small-caps.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Jack Pailing, Non-Executive Chair, acquires 20,000 shares via associated fund at 74.78p per share.
summary: Eschler Global Fund SPC - Colebrooke Opportunities Fund SP, a person closely associated with Non-Executive Chair Jack Pailing, purchased 20,000 ordinary shares of 7.5p each on 23 July 2026 at £0.7478 per share, totalling £14,956. Following this acquisition, Pailing's beneficial interest (including associated parties) comprises 1,084,593 shares, representing approximately 1.51% of issued share capital. The transaction occurred on the London Stock Exchange AIM market.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of director-linked share acquisition; modest position increase suggesting confidence but not material enough to signal strategic intent. Likely read as non-event.

headline: Additional Listing
brief: Next 15 Group issuing 271,140 new shares on AIM as consideration for M. Booth & Associates LLC long-term incentive vesting.
summary: Next 15 Group plc has applied to admit 271,140 new ordinary shares (2.5p each) to AIM in connection with long-term incentive vesting for M. Booth & Associates LLC. The new shares will rank pari passu with existing shares. Following issuance, total ordinary shares in issue will be 102,131,239. Admission is expected by 28 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine share issuance for incentive vesting; modest dilution (~0.27%) unlikely to materially concern investors. Standard practice for equity-based compensation.

headline: Publication of Circular and Notice of GM
brief: Windar Photonics seeks £4m fundraise after discovering €2.8m accounting irregularities in Chinese distributor sales.
summary: Wind sensor technology company Windar Photonics has identified erroneous sales orders worth €2.8m from Chinese distributors in FY24-25, requiring FY25 revenue restatement from €6.4m to €5.0m and a €1.4m receivables provision. The company is seeking approximately £4m (minimum £3.2m net) to fund 18 months of operations, with £2.0m earmarked for working capital and process improvements. FY26 revenue guidance slashed to €1.8m due to delayed major orders from China and US worth over €6m. General Meeting scheduled 7 August 2026; shares remain suspended pending audited results publication. New auditor Buzzacott appointed after previous auditor's resignation.
retail_take: Discovery of accounting irregularities, significant revenue downgrades, and reliance on shareholder approval for survival funding likely signals material distress; market will view negatively despite management's confidence in long-term prospects.

headline: Annual Report and Accounts and Notice of AGM
brief: Baltic Classifieds Group PLC issues 2026 Annual Report and AGM notice; AGM scheduled for 23 September 2026 in Vilnius.
summary: Baltic Classifieds Group PLC (LSE: BCG), the leading online classifieds operator in the Baltics, has published its Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 30 April 2026, reporting €88.5m in revenue across 14 portals operating in Lithuania, Estonia, and Latvia. The company operates four business lines: auto, real estate, jobs & services, and generalist classifieds, employing 163 full-time equivalent staff. The 2026 AGM will be held on 23 September 2026 at 11:00 am local time in Vilnius, with shareholders able to access all documentation via the FCA's National Storage Mechanism.
retail_take: Routine disclosure; no material read-through. Standard annual results publication and AGM notice carry no apparent operational surprises or strategic announcements beyond statutory filing requirements.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Slater Investments reduces STV Group stake from 10.68% to 9.07% through share disposal on 22 July 2026.
summary: Slater Investments, controlled by Mark Slater, notified a reduction in voting rights in STV Group PLC from 10.68% to 9.07% following a share disposal on 22 July 2026. The stake fell from 8,235,595 voting rights to 4,235,595 voting rights. The notification was filed on 23 July 2026 in London. No financial instruments or indirect holdings are involved.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major shareholder reducing exposure; no obvious strategic signal unless indicating broader confidence shift, but insufficient context for market impact assessment.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Harwood Capital entities reduce stake in Avingtrans PLC from 6.28% to 5.61% following share disposal on 22 July.
summary: Harwood Capital LLP and associated entity Oryx International Growth Fund Limited reduced their combined voting rights in Avingtrans PLC to 5.605% (2.07m shares) from 6.282%, crossing below the 6% threshold. The notification was filed 23 July 2026 following the threshold crossing on 22 July. Ultimate controlling persons Christopher Harwood and Bernard Mills held the stake through direct shareholdings and indirect holdings via Harwood Capital LLP.
retail_take: Routine holding disclosure; the stake reduction below 6% is a technical threshold event with no obvious strategic implications for the company or other shareholders.

headline: Correction: Director / PDMR shareholding
brief: Standard Chartered corrects PDMR shareholding disclosure for CEO Judy Hsu with previously omitted share sale details.
summary: Standard Chartered issued a correction to a March 2026 director shareholding announcement for Judy Hsu, CEO of Wealth and Retail Banking. The correction adds a sale of 365 shares at £15.678 per share on 3 March 2026, executed to fund income taxes owed from exercising options under the 2013 Sharesave Plan. Ms Hsu exercised 2,071 shares at £4.230 per share under the same plan. The sale was previously omitted from the original RNS announcement.
retail_take: Routine disclosure correction regarding standard option exercise and tax-funded share sale by senior executive. No material implications for investors beyond regulatory compliance clarification.

headline: Update on timing of publication of Annual Accounts
brief: SEEEN plc delays annual results publication to week of 27 July 2026.
summary: SEEEN plc has updated the timing for publication of its audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2026. Following a previous notice dated 30 June 2026, the company now expects to publish results in the week commencing 27 July 2026. The announcement provides contact details for investor enquiries through its nominated adviser Zeus Capital and investor relations firm focusIR.
retail_take: Routine administrative update on results timing. Modest delay from initial guidance suggests standard audit completion processes rather than material concerns, likely a non-event for markets.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Axtmann Holding GmbH crosses 10% voting rights threshold in Critical Metals PLC
summary: German-registered Axtmann Holding GmbH notified on 23 July 2026 that it crossed a major shareholding threshold in Critical Metals PLC on 22 July, acquiring 10,400,000 voting rights representing 10.22% of the company. This represents an increase from the previous notification level of 9.24%. The holding comprises direct voting rights only, with no financial instruments involved.
retail_take: Routine major holdings disclosure indicating a significant shareholder stake change; typical regulatory filing with no apparent strategic implications unless part of broader acquisition activity.

headline: Final Terms
brief: Xtrackers ETC PLC issues 106,000 gold-backed securities (Tranche 660) at USD 55.98 per unit, maturing April 2080.
summary: Xtrackers ETC PLC issued 106,000 Physical Gold ETC Securities on 24 July 2026 under its Precious Metal Linked Securities Programme, priced at USD 55.976052 per security with total proceeds of USD 5.93m. Each security entitles holders to 0.015375875 troy ounces of gold held in allocated custody by JPMorgan Chase. The securities carry a 0.11% annual base fee, mature 23 April 2080, and are secured by underlying metal holdings. Securities listed on Frankfurt, Borsa Italiana, and London Stock Exchanges.
retail_take: Routine issuance announcement for existing commodity-linked programme; demonstrates ongoing investor demand for physically-backed gold exposure. No material operational or structural changes; standard disclosure for ETC market.

company: Gore Street Energy Storage Fund PLC
headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Bank of America Corporation notifies 2.22% holding in Gore Street Energy Storage Fund PLC via financial instruments.
summary: Bank of America Corporation crossed a notification threshold on 21 July 2026, holding 2.215481% of voting rights in Gore Street Energy Storage Fund PLC (11.19m voting rights). The entire position is held through cash-settled swaps expiring 11 December 2026, with no direct share ownership. This represents a decrease from the previous notification level of 3.081210%. The notification was filed on 22 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a derivative-based holding below material threshold levels; the declining position suggests reduced exposure rather than strategic accumulation, likely a non-event for retail investors.

headline: Director Dealing
brief: Hercules PLC Non-Executive Director Martin Tedham acquires 285,160 shares at 34.75p, raising stake to 13.57%.
summary: Martin Tedham, Non-Executive Director of Hercules plc (AIM: HERC), purchased 285,160 ordinary shares at 34.75 pence per share on 22 July 2026. Following this transaction, Tedham's beneficial holding increases to 10,934,648 shares, representing 13.57% of issued share capital. The purchase was disclosed under EU Market Abuse Regulation requirements.
retail_take: Director share purchase signals confidence in company value, though modest stake increase from established holder; routine but potentially modest positive signal.

headline: Important notice to Shareholders of Xtrackers
Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Jardine Matheson repurchased 12,000 shares at average price of US$64.42, to be cancelled.
summary: On 23 July 2026, JMH repurchased 12,000 ordinary shares at prices ranging from US$64.15 to US$64.98, with a weighted average purchase price of US$64.4176. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, JMH's issued share capital comprises 294,025,976 ordinary shares with one vote each; the company holds no treasury shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback representing modest capital management activity (0.004% of issued shares). No material impact expected; standard capital allocation practice.

headline: Correction of Announcement Released on 22 July 26
brief: DCI Advisors corrects prior announcement regarding tax liability treatment on Cyprus tax receivables from Mr Aristodemou.
summary: DCI Advisors issued a correction to its 22 July 2026 announcement regarding transactions involving tax clearances from Cyprus authorities and settlement of taxes. The original text stated the company had made a provision for estimated taxes due. The correction clarifies that tax liabilities associated with receipts from Mr Aristodemou are uncertain, removing the assertion of a specific provision. All other information in the original announcement remains unchanged.
retail_take: Minor technical correction removing certainty around tax provision estimates; suggests potential downside risk to valuations if actual tax liabilities exceed or differ materially from expectations. Likely a non-event for equity pricing but indicates valuation uncertainty.

headline: Grant of Options
brief: Anglo Asian Mining grants 150,000 share options to Non-Executive Director Cameron Pforr at 412.50p per share.
summary: Anglo Asian Mining PLC granted 150,000 ordinary share options to Cameron Pforr, Non-Executive Director, with an exercise price of 412.50 pence per option. The options vest 50% on 21 July 2027 and 50% on 21 July 2028, granted under the company's existing share option scheme. Anglo Asian is an AIM-listed gold, copper and silver producer in Azerbaijan transitioning to a mid-tier copper-focused producer, producing 25,061 ounces of gold and 7,915 tonnes of copper in FY25.
retail_take: Routine director incentive scheme disclosure. Option strike price of 412.50p signals management confidence in valuation but represents standard compensation practice; minimal market impact expected.

company: Hon Hai Precision Industry Co Ld
headline: FII announcement company sets for dividend day
brief: Hon Hai subsidiary Foxconn Industrial Internet declares cash dividend of RMB 0.65 per share, totalling RMB 12.9bn.
summary: Foxconn Industrial Internet Co., Ltd., a subsidiary of Hon Hai Precision Industry, has declared a cash dividend of RMB 0.65 per share, totalling approximately RMB 12.9 billion. The ex-dividend trading date is set for 3 August 2026, with the record date on 31 July 2026. Payment is scheduled for the same date as the ex-dividend trading date.
retail_take: Routine dividend announcement signalling cash generation capability at the subsidiary level; modest yield context needed to assess whether market views this as positive capital allocation or standard payout.

headline: Result of Annual General Meeting
brief: Petrel Resources AGM held 23 July 2026; all resolutions passed without detail disclosed.
summary: Petrel Resources plc held its Annual General Meeting on 23 July 2026 at Teeling Whiskey Distillery in Dublin. All resolutions presented in the notice of AGM were duly passed. Full details of the resolutions are available in the Annual Report and Notice of AGM on the company's website, though specific resolution details are not itemised in this announcement.
retail_take: Routine AGM confirmation with all resolutions approved; lacks substance on what was actually voted on, making this a procedural disclosure rather than material news for investors.

headline: Directorate change
brief: Andy Halford appointed Chair of RELX Audit Committee effective 23 July 2026. Suzanne Wood steps down from committee.
summary: Andy Halford has been appointed Chair of RELX PLC's Audit Committee, effective following the publication of first-half 2026 results on 23 July 2026. Suzanne Wood has stepped down from the Audit Committee but remains on the Board as Senior Independent Director and continues as a member of the Remuneration, Nominations and Corporate Governance Committees.
retail_take: Routine board committee reshuffle with experienced director taking senior audit oversight role; no material implications for investors or strategy.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Hongkong Land repurchased 450,000 shares at average price of US$7.64, to be cancelled.
summary: Hongkong Land Holdings completed a share repurchase on 22 July 2026, acquiring 450,000 ordinary shares at prices ranging from US$7.51 to US$7.74 per share, with a weighted average purchase price of US$7.6404. The repurchased shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, the company's issued share capital comprises 2,132,296,626 ordinary shares with one vote each and holds no treasury shares.
retail_take: Share buyback typically signals management confidence in valuation and supports earnings per share; modest scale (0.02% of outstanding shares) suggests routine capital management rather than major strategic signal.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Baltic Classifieds repurchased 1.73m shares at 184p average price during 16-22 July 2026 for cancellation.
summary: Baltic Classifieds Group PLC bought back 1,733,764 ordinary shares through Merrill Lynch International between 16-22 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 184.1p per share, ranging from 180p to 189.4p. Shares were purchased across five trading days for cancellation, reducing issued share count to 424.23m. The buyback was executed across multiple venues (LSE, Chi-X, BATS, Turquoise) with detailed transaction data filed per MAR regulations.
retail_take: Routine capital management; share buyback for cancellation reduces share count, potentially accretive to EPS but signals no major strategic issues or undervaluation concerns. Standard disciplinary execution.

headline: Notification of major holdings
brief: Michael Kelly's holding in Poolbeg Pharma fell below 6% threshold, from 6.14% to 5.67%, triggering disclosure notification.
summary: Michael Kelly notified on 23 July 2026 that his voting rights in Poolbeg Pharma PLC decreased to 5.67% (43.3 million shares, ISIN GB00BKPG7Z60) as of 21 July 2026, down from 6.14% previously. The threshold crossing was due to an 'other' event rather than share acquisition or disposal. Kelly holds no financial instruments related to the issuer.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholder's stake reduction below the 6% notification threshold; suggests potential selling activity but no material market implications absent further context.

headline: Buyback programme: transactions 16-22July
brief: Banco Santander repurchased 13m shares (16-22 July) at €11.79-€12.13, bringing total buyback to 80.6% of budget.
summary: Santander executed 13 million share purchases during 16-22 July 2026 across Madrid (XMAD) and Euronext (CEUX) exchanges at weighted average prices ranging €11.79-€12.13. Cumulative buyback spend reached €4.05bn (80.6% of €5.03bn programme cap), representing approximately 17.5% of 2021 outstanding shares. Programme was authorised by Board in February 2026 under EU market abuse regulations.
retail_take: Standard capital return execution progressing toward completion. Consistent pricing and steady pace suggest orderly execution; near 81% utilisation implies programme nearing conclusion. Neutral-to-positive signal of shareholder-friendly capital allocation in normal market conditions.

headline: TR-1: Notification of Major Holdings
brief: Brett Gordon increases Cambridge Cognition stake to 17.9% from 15.6% via acquisition of voting rights.
summary: Brett Gordon, based in Hong Kong, crossed a major shareholding threshold in Cambridge Cognition Holdings PLC on 13 July 2026. His voting rights increased from 15.55% to 17.90%, representing 9,675,000 shares. The notification was filed on 23 July 2026. No financial instruments or indirect holdings were involved. Gordon is not controlled by any other party.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding acquisition. Indicates continued confidence from significant shareholder but no material strategic announcement; regulatory filing with limited immediate market impact.

headline: New members to the Executive Committee
Fingrid group’s half-year report 1.1.–30.6.2026
brief: Fingrid's H1 2026 profit surged 36% to €146m on 20% revenue growth driven by higher consumption and tariffs.
summary: Finland's transmission operator reported H1 2026 turnover of €688m (up 20% YoY) and net profit of €146m (up 36%), supported by 6.3% electricity consumption growth to 46.1 TWh, higher grid tariffs, and imbalance pricing. Operating profit excluding derivatives was €165m. Capital expenditure totalled €198m with €2bn planned for 2026-2029. Strong pipeline: 68GW consumption enquiries, 3GW+ data centre connections signed, 3GW+ electric boilers, 4GW storage projects. Balance service fees were cut thrice to €0.40/MWh effective July. Finnish State ownership increased to 59.5%, OP Pohjola to 14.2%.
retail_take: Strong operational and financial performance with robust growth momentum from data centre demand and rising tariffs, offset somewhat by regulatory disputes ongoing in courts and execution risks on major transmission projects.

headline: Directorate change
brief: Ceiba Investments appoints Enrique Martinon Garcia as non-executive director, effective immediately.
summary: Enrique Martinon Garcia, CEO of Grupo Martinon (a hospitality and real estate group operating across Spain, Caribbean, and Latin America), joins Ceiba's board as non-executive director and Management Engagement Committee member. Martinon Garcia brings 30+ years' international hospitality sector experience in hotel development, financing, and asset management. His investment vehicle GRUMASA already holds 27.5 million shares in Ceiba, a Cuba-focused real estate investment company.
retail_take: Appointment reinforces board expertise in hospitality/real estate sectors aligned with Ceiba's Cuba investment strategy; existing significant shareholder adds credibility but represents continuity rather than material news.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: JPMorgan Chase notifies 7.02% holding in Senior PLC via direct shares and equity swaps.
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. crossed a notification threshold in Senior PLC on 20 July 2026, holding 7.016522% total voting rights as of that date. The position comprises 0.596421% in direct shares (2.5m voting rights) and 6.420101% through cash-settled equity swaps expiring between October 2026 and September 2027. J.P. Morgan Securities PLC is the direct holder. The notification was filed 22 July 2026 following the threshold crossing.
retail_take: Routine major shareholding disclosure; JPMorgan's position appears primarily derivative-based and short-term in nature, typical of trading desk activity rather than strategic investment intent.

headline: Overseas Regulatory Announcement - Board Meeting
brief: HSBC board meeting scheduled for 4 August 2026 to approve interim results and second interim dividend for 2026.
summary: HSBC Holdings PLC will hold a Board committee meeting on 4 August 2026 to consider its interim results for the six months ended 30 June 2026 and approval of a second interim dividend for 2026. Subject to Board approval, the dividend will be payable on 25 September 2026 to shareholders of record on 14 August 2026 across UK, Hong Kong, Bermuda registers and American Depositary Shareholders. Full dividend details will be announced with the results announcement.
