retail_take: Routine buyback execution under previously announced programme; indicates share repurchase continuation but typically signals no major strategic developments or earnings catalyst.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Greencoat Renewables repurchased 250,014 shares at €0.7570-€0.7670 for cancellation on 22 July 2026.
summary: Greencoat Renewables PLC purchased 250,014 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin on 22 July 2026 as part of its share buyback programme announced 5 March 2026. Shares were bought at prices ranging from €0.7570 to €0.7670, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7625. Following settlement, the company holds 200,000 treasury shares and has 1,082,584,271 shares in issue. All repurchased shares will be cancelled.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within announced buyback programme; share cancellation reduces share count, potentially accretive to EPS, but minor single-day tranche suggests ongoing programme implementation rather than material signal.

headline: Update on Arbitration Proceedings against Ukraine
brief: Enwell Energy secures interim protective order in Ukraine arbitration case under ICSID treaty framework.
summary: Enwell Energy (AIM: ENW) has obtained Procedural Order No. 3 from the ICSID arbitration tribunal (dated 26 June 2026) against Ukraine, following disputes over asset freezes, licence suspensions, and operational interference since December 2022. The agreed interim measures require both parties to notify the tribunal within four days of new proceedings affecting Enwell's rights, with reminder of good-faith obligations. The tribunal was constituted in March 2026; Enwell filed its provisional measures request in October 2025.
retail_take: Procedural progress in investor protection dispute; interim order provides some safeguard against further adverse action but represents nascent arbitration stage. Outcome remains highly uncertain and distant; unlikely material positive or negative catalyst near-term.

headline: Issue of Equity in lieu of past director’s fees
brief: Shuka Minerals issues 375,000 shares at 4p each to settle former director's accrued fees.
summary: Shuka Minerals PLC will issue 375,000 ordinary shares priced at 4 pence per share to a former director in settlement of accrued fees owed. Admission to AIM expected 29 July 2026, expanding total issued capital to 143,762,497 shares. The equity issuance avoids cash outlay for historical fee obligations while diluting existing shareholders by approximately 0.26%.
retail_take: Minor dilutive event settling past liabilities through equity rather than cash—routine corporate housekeeping unlikely to move the market materially either way.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Mercia Asset Management repurchased 135,000 shares at 26.50p per share during 16-22 July 2026 under buyback programme.
summary: Mercia Asset Management PLC purchased 135,000 ordinary shares between 16-22 July 2026 at a consistent price of 26.50p, executing its buyback programme announced 1 April 2026. The shares will be cancelled. Following these purchases, issued shares total 421,744,557 (excluding 11.6m treasury shares). The asset manager manages £2.2bn in assets under management and operates 11 regional offices focused on UK SME capital provision.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution at stable pricing; suggests management confidence in valuation but represents modest capital allocation with no material near-term implications for shareholders.

headline: Half Year Report
brief: Howden Joinery H1 2026: Sales +3.3%, underlying operating profit +5.5%, interim dividend +2% to 5.1p. DIY Kitchens acquisition completed post-period.
summary: Howden Joinery delivered H1 2026 sales of £1,030.6m (+3.3% YoY) with underlying operating profit of £128.1m (+5.5%). Gross margin improved 70bps to 62.8% through balanced pricing, volumes and cost efficiencies. Underlying EPS grew 5.5% to 17.3p. Company completed £390m acquisition of DIY Kitchens on 23 June 2026, an online kitchen business generating £136m revenue with 17% five-year growth. Interim dividend increased 2% to 5.1p. Management confirmed full-year 2026 outlook unchanged and £100m share buyback programme on track.
retail_take: Solid operational performance with margin expansion and disciplined cost management supports confidence in guidance. DIY Kitchens acquisition signals growth diversification into complementary online channel, expected accretive immediately. Strong cash generation (£208.3m operating cash flow) and maintained net cash position post-acquisition support shareholder returns.

headline: New Natural Hydrogen Contract Win
brief: Getech wins €1m+ European Commission contract to map natural hydrogen potential across EU.
summary: Getech has been awarded a major European Commission contract worth more than €1 million over FY26-FY27 as part of a consortium led by Trinomics. The 12-month study, commissioned by DG GROW, will map underground natural hydrogen reservoirs across all EU Member States, assess commercial extraction feasibility, and provide regulatory recommendations. Getech will deliver geological components using its proprietary gravity/magnetic datasets and Globe platform, generating revenue through consultancy, data and software licensing fees.
retail_take: Financially material contract validates Getech's natural hydrogen expertise and positions it as a thought leader; opens potential for similar studies globally, supporting long-term strategic positioning in emerging energy transition.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IWG repurchased 153,418 shares on 22 July 2026 as part of ongoing £300m+ buyback programme announced December 2025.
summary: International Workplace Group plc purchased 153,418 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 at volume-weighted average price of £1.9692, via Jefferies International Limited across multiple venues. Since programme launch on 31 December 2025, the company has repurchased 40.5m shares. Post-purchase, 953.8m shares remain in issue (excluding treasury). Shares will be cancelled or held in treasury per shareholder approval at May 2026 AGM.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within approved buyback remit; neutral signal absent broader context on buyback rationale, cash position, or strategic intent. Market typically reads share repurchases as modestly positive (EPS accretion, capital flexibility) but immaterial absent strategic or valuation catalysts.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Experian repurchased 28,495 shares on 22 July at average price of 2,682p per share; shares to be cancelled.
summary: Under its June 2026 share repurchase programme, Experian purchased 28,495 ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange via BofA Securities Europe SA on 22 July 2026. Shares traded between 2,652p and 2,699p, with a weighted average price of 2,682.26p. The purchased shares will be cancelled. Transactions are published weekly on the company website.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within announced programme; capital return demonstrates confidence but has minimal market impact as regular-course repurchases are well-telegraphed.

headline: Metir Secures Qatar Project Confirmation Update
brief: Metir secures Qatar water monitoring project confirmation with 10-year commitment and expansion prospects.
summary: Modern Water's Continuous Toxicity Monitoring project in Qatar has received formal confirmation from Kahramaa and regional partner Avanceon. Phase 1 handover is reset to October 2026, releasing ~£205,000 in outstanding payments. Post-handover, the contract transitions to recurring consumables revenue (~£30,000/month from 27 units; >£50,000/month if Phase 2's proposed 17 additional systems proceed). Kahramaa committed to 10-year project duration with plans for local reagent production facility.
retail_take: Positive catalyst converting uncertain project into predictable recurring revenue stream with expansion optionality; resolves prior technical concerns and validates core technology in strategically important Middle East market.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Babcock International repurchased 13,548 shares on 22 July 2026 at average price of 1,107.22p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Babcock International Group PLC purchased 13,548 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities plc at prices ranging from 1,074.5p to 1,124.5p, with volume-weighted average price of 1,107.22p. Since 1 July 2026, the company has repurchased 886,370 shares costing £8.97m. The company holds 13.46m treasury shares with 492.14m shares in issue.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within disclosed programme; demonstrates capital discipline and potential EPS accretion, though market likely views as non-material update to previously-announced strategy.

headline: Issue of Shares
brief: Landore Resources issues 880,921 shares worth £16,737.50 to joint broker Hannam & Partners as fee payment.
summary: Landore Resources Limited (AIM: LND) has issued 880,921 ordinary shares to settle fees owed to joint broker Hannam & Partners totalling £16,737.50. The shares will be admitted to AIM trading on or around 28 July 2026. Following admission, the company's total issued share capital will comprise 372,303,404 ordinary shares with voting rights, with no treasury shares held.
retail_take: Routine equity issuance for advisory fees; modest dilution and non-material transaction; typical for small-cap AIM companies managing broker relationships.

headline: Pre-Close Trading Update
brief: Volution expects adjusted EPS of 38.0p, 4% above consensus, with 15% revenue growth and margin expansion across all regions.
summary: Volution Group forecasts adjusted EPS of approximately 38.0p for FY26 ending 31 July 2026, representing 15% growth versus prior year and outperforming market consensus of 36.4p. Total revenue growth of c.15% driven by organic growth of c.3% constant currency plus the AC Industries acquisition in Australasia. All three regions delivered organic operating margin expansion; Continental Europe achieved strongest organic growth of 5.5-6%, while UK remained resilient despite flat full-year revenue. Leverage expected at 1.6x ex-leases, with operating cash conversion above 90% target.
retail_take: Strong pre-close beat on earnings guidance, margin expansion, and maintained organic growth momentum despite challenging conditions signals operational execution strength; market likely views positively as earnings accretion and balance sheet flexibility support continued M&A.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Record PLC shareholders approve all 15 AGM resolutions with >90% support, including final dividend of 1.45p per share.
summary: Record PLC's 22 July 2026 AGM saw all 15 resolutions pass decisively, with voting support exceeding 90% across the board. Key approvals included adoption of 2026 accounts, a final dividend of 1.45 pence per share, re-election of directors Jan Witte, David Morrison, Matt Hotson, and Krystyna Nowak, appointment of Nick Adams as new director, and re-appointment of BDO LLP as auditor. Special resolutions authorising share issuance, pre-emption rights disapplication, and share buyback authority also passed with strong margins (96-99% support). Shareholder attendance represented 47.2% of issued share capital.
retail_take: Strong governance outcome with overwhelming shareholder endorsement across routine and discretionary matters; dividend confirmation and expanded director authority provide management flexibility. Market likely views this as routine positive housekeeping with no material concerns signalled.

headline: Third Quarter Trading Update
brief: Mitchells & Butlers reports resilient 2.2% YTD like-for-like sales growth despite Q3 headwinds from extreme heat and Easter timing.
summary: The pub and restaurant operator delivered 2.2% year-to-date like-for-like sales growth through 42 weeks to 18 July 2026, though Q3 saw flat performance due to ~0.8ppt Easter timing headwind and extreme heat damaging food-led brands like Toby Carvery and Miller & Carter. Drink-led pubs performed better, boosted by World Cup trading. The company completed 181 conversions and acquired 10 new sites. Cost inflation expectations remain at £120m; management remains confident in delivering full-year consensus expectations.
retail_take: Confidence reaffirmed despite near-term trading pressure; diversified portfolio resilience and investment programme offset headwinds, likely viewed as steady-hand stewardship in challenging conditions.

headline: Half Year Trading Update
brief: Mission Group posts in-line H1 performance with 10% operating profit growth to £2.4m and strong pipeline entering H2.
summary: Mission Group's H1 2026 revenue reached £32.5m (down from £34.1m H1 2025) with headline operating profit up 10% to £2.4m and headline PBT up 27% to £1.4m. The simplified agency portfolio integration and restructuring programme have driven margin improvement. Net bank debt stands at £11.0m (up from £9.0m year-end due to acquisition settlements and restructuring costs). The group maintains confidence in meeting full-year market expectations of £70.0m revenue and £8.1m headline operating profit.
retail_take: Likely read as positive: operating profit and PBT growth despite revenue decline shows successful cost restructuring, while strong pipeline and management confidence support full-year guidance. However, rising debt and revenue decline warrant monitoring.

headline: Update on Offer for Accent
brief: Frasers Group extends takeover offer for Accent Group to 30 September 2026 at A$0.65 per share.
summary: Frasers Group's all-cash takeover offer for Australian-listed Accent Group Limited, announced 15 June 2026 at A$0.65 per share, has been extended to 30 September 2026. The extension was announced via the ASX on 23 July 2026 by Frasers' broker Barrenjoey Markets. No other terms of the offer have changed. The extension provides additional time for acceptance of the bid.
retail_take: Extension suggests slower-than-expected acceptances or regulatory delays, neither validating the offer price nor signalling problems; likely a procedural non-event for most investors.

headline: Preliminary Results - 52 weeks ended 3 May 2026
brief: TheWorks reports 47% EBITDA growth to £14.0m on 3.3% LFL sales growth; FY27 guidance maintained at £15.0m.
summary: TheWorks delivered FY26 pre-IFRS 16 Adjusted EBITDA of £14.0m (FY25: £9.5m), a 47% increase, on total revenue of £260m with like-for-like sales growth of 3.3%, significantly outperforming the UK non-food retail market decline of 0.1%. Product margin improved 240bps through supplier negotiations and better stock control; the company achieved its planned £2m cost reduction programme. The company closed its loss-making online business in March 2026 (under 10% of sales) to focus on its 508-store estate. Maintained FY27 Adjusted EBITDA guidance of £15.0m with strong momentum continuing, posting 8.8% LFL growth in the first 11 weeks of FY27.
retail_take: Market likely to view positively: strong profit growth, outperformance versus sector, disciplined strategic pivot to profitable stores, and positive early-year trading momentum suggest sustainable recovery in profitability.

headline: FY2027 Q1 performance update
brief: 3i Group reports strong Q1 FY2027 with NAV up 3% to 3,131p; Action continues impressive growth with 14% sales increase.
summary: 3i Group's NAV per share increased to 3,131 pence at 30 June 2026 from 3,030 pence, delivering 3% total return despite £276m FX headwind. Flagship holding Action generated €8,348m net sales and €1,108m operating EBITDA (both 14% and 13% ahead YoY respectively) in the first six months, with 3.6% like-for-like growth. Action added 121 net new stores to reach 3,423 locations and remains on track for 400+ store additions this year. 3i's portfolio also benefited from a £254m dividend from Action and £18m from infrastructure holding 3iN. The group launched a £750m share buyback in May 2026, with £344m deployed by 21 July.
retail_take: Strong operational performance from Action and stable portfolio momentum likely viewed positively by markets, though FX headwinds and valuation multiples unchanged suggest measured investor sentiment; buyback signals confidence in capital allocation.

headline: Q3 TRADING UPDATE
brief: ATG raises FY26 revenue guidance to 5.5-6.5% on strong Q3 Arts & Antiques growth and Chairish integration progress.
summary: Auction Technology Group reported Q3 pro forma constant currency revenue growth of 7.8%, with Arts & Antiques showing good momentum from LiveAuctioneers and Chairish, while Industrial & Commercial continued declining at consistent rates. The group upgraded full-year FY26 guidance from 5.0-6.0% to 5.5-6.5% revenue growth, maintaining adjusted EBITDA margin guidance of 34.5-35.5% with leverage expected to improve to 1.5x by year-end from 2.2x in FY25, reflecting healthy cash generation.
retail_take: Market likely sees this positively: upgraded guidance, successful portfolio integration delivering synergies, and strengthened balance sheet signal operational execution and strategic progress, though lower-margin service growth and I&C decline warrant monitoring.

headline: RELX 2026 INTERIM RESULTS
brief: RELX reports H1 2026 results with 7% underlying revenue growth and 9% profit growth; reaffirms full-year outlook.
summary: RELX delivered H1 2026 revenue of £4,871m (underlying growth +7%) and adjusted operating profit of £1,727m (underlying growth +9%), with adjusted EPS of 68.6p, constant currency growth +11%. The interim dividend increased 7% to 20.9p. Operating margin improved to 35.5% from 34.8%. All business segments contributed growth: Risk +8%, Scientific/Technical/Medical +6%, Legal +10%, Exhibitions +6%. Net debt/EBITDA stands at 2.3x. The company completed £1,750m of a £2,250m share buyback and two acquisitions totalling £103m. Management expects continued strong underlying growth in revenue and profit for the full year.
retail_take: Strong operational momentum with double-digit profit growth at constant currency, margin expansion, and confident full-year guidance should support positive sentiment. AI-driven product innovation and consistent capital returns underscore management execution.

headline: Final Results
brief: Naked Wines reports FY26 adjusted EBITDA of £7.6m, ahead of guidance, with £33.4m net cash and continued profitability focus.
summary: Naked Wines delivered adjusted EBITDA of £7.6m (up 35% constant currency vs FY25's £6.7m), ahead of £5.5m-£7.5m guidance. Revenue declined 20% to £199.1m as the company deliberately reduced unprofitable customer acquisition. Gross margin expanded to 19.9% from 18.4%. Net cash rose to £33.4m (from £30.1m) despite £6m share buybacks. FY27 guidance: revenue £158m-£175m, EBITDA £7.6m-£9.0m, net cash £34m-£42m. Company has delivered £25m annualised savings against £23m medium-term target.
retail_take: Market likely views positively: company prioritising profitability over growth through disciplined cost-cutting and selective customer acquisition, with strong balance sheet and shareholder returns, though deliberate revenue decline signals challenge ahead.

headline: First Quarter AUM Update
brief: Record PLC reports Q1 AUM of $122.0bn, up 6% QoQ, with positive net flows across all three product pillars.
summary: Record PLC's assets under management reached $122.0 billion at 30 June 2026, up from $114.6 billion on 31 March 2026. Net flows totalled $2.3 billion across Risk Management ($0.7bn), Absolute Return ($0.6bn), and Private Markets ($1.0bn). Asset movements contributed $5.4 billion positively, partially offset by $0.3 billion FX headwinds. Risk Management grew to $98.7 billion, Absolute Return to $4.4 billion, and Private Markets to $18.9 billion. CEO Jan Witte cited strength in Passive Hedging and new FX Alpha mandates, with undeployed commitments of $1.2 billion.
retail_take: Solid organic and market-driven growth across all divisions signals healthy demand and operational momentum, likely viewed positively as AUM drives fee revenues and validates management's strategic direction.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IHG repurchased 1,000 shares at $154.25-$156.10 on 22 July under existing authority; shares to be cancelled.
summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 1,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 via Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from $154.25 to $156.10, with a volume-weighted average price of $155.49. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, IHG has 148.6 million shares in issue, excluding 5.4 million treasury shares. The repurchase was conducted under shareholder authority granted at the May 2025 AGM.
retail_take: Minimal capital impact given the small 1,000-share purchase size relative to the 154m share base; routine share buyback continuation under existing authority; no material signal for investors.

headline: Management Appointments
brief: Empire Metals appoints General Manager (Project Delivery) and Senior Advisor (Approvals) to accelerate Pitfield titanium project development.
summary: Empire Metals appointed Chris Dodds as General Manager (Project Delivery) and Emma Neale as Senior Advisor (Approvals) to advance the Pitfield Titanium Project toward commercial development. Dodds brings 20+ years' experience across mining and critical minerals sectors with roles at Alcoa, Iluka Resources, and Wood. Neale has 14+ years' environmental approvals experience in Western Australia and internationally. The appointments strengthen the in-house team to progress feasibility studies; Pitfield hosts a 2.2 billion tonne resource grading 5.1% TiO₂.
retail_take: Strategic bolstering of operational capacity signals management confidence in project timeline, likely viewed positively as de-risking execution, though remains development-stage without near-term revenue catalysts.

headline: Issue of Equity and TVR
brief: Rockwood Strategic issues 100,000 shares at 322.89p per share for cash via blocklisting facility.
summary: Rockwood Strategic PLC issued 100,000 ordinary shares of 5p each at 322.89p per share on 24 July 2026 from its blocklisting facility, raising approximately £322,890. Post-issuance, total issued share capital comprises 60,868,760 ordinary shares with no treasury holdings, establishing the voting rights denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules notifications.
retail_take: Modest equity raise via established facility; routine capital raising with no apparent strategic significance or dilution concerns given share count context.

headline: AGM Statement
brief: Vp plc AGM statement confirms resilient Q1 trading and reiterates full-year guidance in line with analyst expectations.
summary: Equipment rental specialist Vp plc held its AGM on 23 July 2026 with Chairman Jeremy Pilkington stating Q1 trading was resilient despite slower UK Infrastructure activity. The group expects activity to build as AMP8 investment programmes mobilise. Analyst consensus for 2026/2027 forecasts revenue of ££351.3m and profit before tax (excluding amortisation and exceptional items) of £32.5m. Management confirmed completion of Brandon Hire Station restructuring and ongoing digital transformation investment, with confidence in capitalising on long-term specialist market opportunities.
retail_take: Management reaffirms guidance amid mixed near-term headwinds (slower infrastructure) offset by strategic progress and medium-term growth drivers, suggesting cautious but steady outlook that meets rather than exceeds market expectations.

headline: Senior Finance and Exploration Appointments
brief: Cobra Resources appoints CFO Cormac Byrne and Exploration Manager Andrew Alesci to strengthen team.
summary: South Australian critical minerals developer Cobra Resources has appointed Cormac Byrne as Chief Financial Officer, transitioning from consulting, with prior CFO experience at Centrex Limited and background in corporate banking and project finance. Andrew Alesci joins as Exploration Manager with 17+ years' South Australian and Northern Territory exploration experience across multiple deposit types. The appointments support parallel development of Boland (rare earth in situ recovery project) and Manna Hill (copper-gold-molybdenum) projects, with maiden rare earth resource estimate expected to catalyse Boland's economic evaluation phase.
retail_take: Senior hires signal progression toward development stage for flagship projects, particularly the rare earth Boland discovery. Market likely views positively as evidence of execution capability and transition from exploration toward economics phase.

headline: AGM Statement
brief: Inspiration Healthcare reports strong FY26 growth; Chair signals continued momentum into FY27 at AGM.
summary: FY26 revenue grew 24% to £47.5m with gross profit up 27% to £20.8m and adjusted EBITDA of £2.8m. Operating cash inflow reached £7.5m with net debt reduced 39%. SLE business showed strong growth including major international export orders. Post-year-end, the company transferred its infusion business to Micrel Medical Devices to focus on neonatal medtech. Chair Roy Davis noted positive momentum continuing into FY27 with a strengthened order book and opportunity pipeline.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively: double-digit revenue growth, improved profitability, strong cash generation and strategic focus on higher-growth neonatal business should appeal to growth-oriented investors on AIM.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: First Abu Dhabi Bank publishes Q2 2026 half-year financial results with interim financials, management analysis and earnings presentation.
summary: First Abu Dhabi Bank P.J.S.C. released its second quarter 2026 financial results on 23 July 2026. The disclosure includes condensed consolidated interim financial statements for the period ended 30 June 2026, management discussion and analysis, and an earnings presentation. All documents are available on FAB's investor relations website. The announcement is a routine quarterly filing containing standard financial reporting materials without specific earnings figures or performance metrics detailed in the announcement text itself.
retail_take: Routine quarterly disclosure announcement. Market impact depends on actual financial results within the published documents; the RNS filing itself is a procedural notification without material figures or commentary.

headline: Publication of a Prospectus
brief: CBA publishes FCA-approved prospectus for USD 40bn covered bond programme dated 20 July 2026.
summary: Commonwealth Bank of Australia has published an FCA-approved base prospectus for its USD 40 billion covered bond programme. The programme allows CBA to issue covered bonds in multiple currencies, guaranteed by Perpetual Corporate Trust Limited as trustee of the CBA Covered Bond Trust. Bonds may be issued on a continuing basis to designated dealers and listed on the London Stock Exchange. CBA maintains AA- (S&P), Aa2 (Moody's) and AA (Fitch) ratings. This prospectus supersedes the prior version dated 22 July 2025.
retail_take: Routine capital markets disclosure enabling CBA to issue covered bonds in UK markets. Standard funding mechanism with no material news; market likely treats as non-event.

headline: Nestlé and Platinum Equity to create Peranel
Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Avation repurchases 10,000 shares at 135p per share under approved buyback programme.
summary: Aircraft leasing company Avation PLC repurchased 10,000 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 at 135 pence per share, to be held in treasury. Following the transaction, 61.4m shares remain in issue with 1.6m in treasury, reducing voting rights to 59.8m. The buyback operates under a mandate approved at the November 2025 AGM, authorising repurchase of up to 25% of issued share capital within specified price limits until the next AGM.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under existing authorisation; modest transaction size suggests measured capital deployment, typically neutral-to-slightly-positive as it reduces share count and signals confidence, though immaterial at this volume.

headline: Proposed Acquisition Update
brief: Golden Rock Global updates on proposed acquisition of SSS Matrix Corp, now rebranded as StarEdge Digital Infrastructure Inc.
summary: Golden Rock Global PLC is progressing with its acquisition of SSS Matrix Corp, which has completed corporate restructuring and rebranded as StarEdge Digital Infrastructure Inc (SEDI). SEDI is a technology business focused on AI-driven computing demands, optimizing energy usage and computing power for datacenters. Revenue streams include quantum security hardware/software procurement, server sales and leasing, maintenance services, and quantum chip sales. The transaction remains subject to definitive agreement execution, due diligence completion, and prospectus publication. Golden Rock expects to appoint a Sponsor shortly.
retail_take: Early-stage acquisition update with operational progress but multiple conditions remaining; limited detail suggests market should await sponsor appointment and prospectus for material assessment.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund repurchased 50,000 own shares at GBP 4.216 per share on 22 July 2026.
summary: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited repurchased 50,000 USD 0.01 ordinary shares (USD Shares) at GBP 4.216 per share on 22 July 2026, to be held as treasury shares. Total treasury shares now stand at 6,730,142 USD Shares, with 120,349,381 USD Shares in issue excluding treasury holdings. The total voting rights in the Company are 120,349,381.
retail_take: Routine share buyback typical for closed-ended investment funds managing capital structure; no immediate market implications for investors.

headline: CLN Conversion and Issue of Advisor Equity
brief: Total Graphite issues 5.96m shares for CLN conversion and advisor fees ahead of LSE admission.
summary: Total Graphite PLC is issuing 5,958,863 ordinary shares: 4,397,000 shares at 1.5p to an advisor as commission for fundraising; and 1,561,863 shares at 3.75p for conversion of £50,000 CLN 2022 Notes with accrued interest. Shares admit to London Stock Exchange Main Market on 28 July 2026. Post-admission, total voting rights will be 768,406,787.
retail_take: Routine capital restructuring ahead of listing. Advisor fee settlement via equity dilution and CLN conversion are standard LSE pre-admission mechanics; no obvious negative signal.

headline: Pre-close Trading Update and Notice of Results
brief: Airea reports H1 2026 sales up 10.2% to £10.81m; new manufacturing facility installation complete.
summary: Airea PLC's H1 2026 (six months ended 30 June) sales reached £10.81m, up 10.2% year-on-year, with volume growth of 7.4% and operating profit ahead of prior year. UK and ROI markets grew 10.2%, while international markets grew 9.9%. The company completed installation of its new manufacturing facility with no material operational disruption during construction. Q3 has started strongly with a robust order book. The company is expanding its sustainable product range, including biogenic yarns from renewable organic waste, showcased at recent Clerkenwell Design Week. Interim results due 29 September 2026.
retail_take: Strong organic growth across all segments, capex cycle completion, and confidence in Q3 momentum suggest positive momentum, though investors will await detailed interim results and manufacturing payoff visibility.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Barings Emerging EMEA Opportunities purchases 911 shares at 820.50p for cancellation.
summary: On 22 July 2026, Barings Emerging EMEA Opportunities PLC bought back 911 ordinary shares at a weighted average price of 820.50 pence per share via J.P. Morgan Securities plc on the London Stock Exchange. The company intends to cancel these shares. Following the transaction, the company holds 3,318,207 treasury shares and has 11,485,303 ordinary shares in issue.
retail_take: Routine share buyback and cancellation; modest scale transaction typical of capital management activity with no material implications for shareholders.

headline: Full Year Trading Update and Notice of Results
brief: Fonix reports 12.9% gross profit growth to £21.0m; expands into four new European markets with positive momentum.
summary: Mobile payments provider Fonix delivered FY26 gross profit of £21.0m (up 12.9% from £18.6m) and adjusted EBITDA of £16.2m (up 11.0% from £14.6m), slightly ahead of market expectations of £20.5m and £16.0m respectively. Total payment volume grew 8.0% to £303.3m. The company extended contracts with ITV and Global, launched commercial operations in Portugal, conducted successful pilots in Switzerland, and established legal entities in France and a fifth European market. Product innovation included CompsPortal's award-winning launch with Channel 5 and RCS trials. Fonix completed a £2.0m share buyback and plans an increased dividend.
retail_take: Steady operational execution with international expansion ahead of market consensus; strong client retention and recurring revenue underpin confidence, though international ramp-up involves execution risk.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gaming Realms buys back 137,252 shares at ~30.93p as part of ongoing programme.
summary: Gaming Realms PLC purchased 137,252 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 via Peel Hunt at prices between 30.80p and 30.98p, with a volume-weighted average of 30.93p. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this transaction, the company holds 24.5m treasury shares and has 271.7m shares in issue, representing total voting rights.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution under announced programme. Share repurchases typically indicate management confidence but are non-events absent context around capital allocation strategy or market conditions.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: Yorkshire Building Society reports H1 2026 profit of £181.9m with strong savings growth but mortgage market pressures.
summary: Yorkshire Building Society delivered statutory profit before tax of £181.9m (H1 2025: £187.9m) with core operating profit of £175.0m. Savings balances grew to £53.3bn with 288,000 new accounts opened; the Society maintained a 0.54pp savings rate advantage over market. Gross mortgage lending reached £4.6bn with 19,300 new residential mortgages, though net lending fell to £0.4bn from £0.9bn year-on-year. Capital ratios remained robust at 19.2% CET1 and 7.1% leverage. NPS declined from 66 to 60 due to service pressures from record transaction volumes.
retail_take: Solid operational performance demonstrates market share gains in savings despite mortgage market headwinds, though margin pressures and service degradation signal structural challenges. Likely read as resilient but cautious.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: GlobalData repurchases 93,945 shares at 73.92p average as part of ongoing buyback programme; shares to be cancelled.
summary: GlobalData purchased 93,945 ordinary shares on 22 July 2026 through Investec at a weighted average price of 73.9185p, with trades ranging from 72.70p to 74.50p. The shares will be cancelled, reducing total voting rights to 745,424,812. This is part of the company's Share Buyback Programme that commenced 23 June 2026. No shares are held in treasury following the transaction.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution reducing share count; modest daily volume suggests measured approach. No material market implications unless programme signals confidence or capital management concerns exist.

headline: AGM Trading Update
brief: Motorpoint reports Q1 FY27 momentum with 11.1% retail volume growth and improved profitability; opens 22nd store.
summary: Motorpoint Group delivered strong Q1 FY27 performance with retail volumes up 11.1% year-on-year, supported by increased stock levels and improved profitability driven by strong metal margins and cost discipline. The company opened its 22nd store, Motorpoint Leeds, on 10 July 2026, with further store openings confirmed. Progress includes improved vehicle supply, direct consumer sales via Sell Your Car channel, and continued investment in data, AI and technology. Non-Executive Director Mary McNamara is stepping down after ten years on the board.
retail_take: Strong organic growth with expanding store footprint and profitability improvements suggests positive momentum; market likely reads this as encouraging trading update ahead of interim results in October.

headline: Martello update - Sakoose Drill Permit Received
brief: Richmond Hill Resources receives drilling permit for Martello Project in Ontario, valid until July 2029.
summary: Richmond Hill Resources has obtained approval for a mechanised core drilling permit from the Ontario Ministry of Energy and Mines for the Martello Project, valid until 20 July 2029. The company plans to work with Critical Discoveries on drill targeting at the Sakoose priority target, with final drill hole locations subject to refinement following assay results from recent field programs and geological analysis.
retail_take: Positive milestone removing regulatory hurdle for exploration; enables next phase of drilling campaign but represents typical development progress rather than material discovery.

headline: Further re: Receipt of Winding Up Petition
brief: ADM Energy's HMRC winding-up petition hearing adjourned 56 days to 16 September 2026; alleged debt reduced by over 50%.
summary: ADM Energy's winding-up petition hearing before the court on 22 July 2026 was adjourned for 56 days, with relisting on 16 September 2026 at the Rolls Building, London (case CR-2026-004486). HMRC confirmed the outstanding amount it alleges is now less than 50% of the initial petition claim. No winding-up order was made. The company continues negotiations with HMRC to reconcile amounts due and agree payment terms. Share trading remains suspended on AIM pending publication of 2025 annual accounts.
retail_take: Modest positive development—significant reduction in alleged debt and no immediate winding-up order suggest breathing room, but unresolved tax dispute and share suspension remain material concerns for investors awaiting resolution.

headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock American Income Trust updates voting rights denominator to 61.66m shares as at 23 July 2026.
summary: BlackRock American Income Trust plc notified the market of its issued share capital as at 23 July 2026, comprising 61,660,138 Ordinary Shares carrying one vote per share. The company holds 33,701,167 shares in treasury. Shareholders must use 61,660,138 as the denominator for calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure updating the voting rights denominator for threshold calculations. No material implications for investors.

headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust confirms 244.5m ordinary shares in issue (excluding treasury stock) as at 23 July 2026.
summary: BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust plc notified the market of its issued capital as at 23 July 2026: 244,453,950 ordinary shares of £0.05 each, excluding 54,238,655 shares held in treasury. This figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating disclosure obligations under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1 (Article 15 Transparency Directive, DTR 5.6).
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of voting rights and issued share capital. No material implications for investors; standard transparency requirement with no read-through to business performance or strategy.

headline: Trading Update for the quarter ended 30 June 2026
brief: easyJet Q3 PBT collapsed 70% to £85m due to Middle East conflict impact and elevated fuel costs, despite operational improvements.
summary: Q3 headline PBT fell to £85m from £286m YoY, driven by £105m fuel cost increase and weaker bookings following March Middle East conflict onset. Passengers: 25.8m with 88.9% load factor (-1ppt). RASK declined 3% YoY though improved 1ppt since May. Q4 currently 68% sold with flat yields; Q1'27 shows encouraging mid-single digit yield growth. easyJet holidays delivered £84m PBT with 7% profit growth ex-FX. Management changes: COO David Morgan retiring; Sophie Dekkers promoted to COO; Daniel Skjeldam joins as CCO September 1st.
retail_take: Significant earnings miss reflects external headwinds rather than operational failures—operational metrics actually improved. However, full-year guidance dependent on volatile fuel prices and bookings recovery remains uncertain, limiting positive sentiment.

headline: OP Pohjola's Half-year Financial Report 1 January–30 June 2026: Operating profit for January–June EUR 831 million – excellent second quarter
OP Corporate Bank plc's Half-year Financial Report 1 January–30 June 2026
Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pershing Square Holdings repurchased 33,970 shares on 22 July 2026 at average price of 51.12 USD.
summary: PSH purchased 33,970 public shares through Jefferies on 22 July 2026: 23,309 shares (PSH ticker) at average 3,824 pence/51.14 USD and 10,661 shares (PSHD ticker) at average 51.08 USD. NAV per share was 76.01 USD as of 21 July. Following the buyback, 173.7 million shares remain outstanding with 37.3 million held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within ongoing programme; buyback at discount to NAV typically favourable to remaining shareholders, though modest scale suggests non-event for market.

headline: NBPE Announces Transaction in Own Shares
brief: NBPE buys back 24,848 Class A shares at £14.32-£14.64, reducing outstanding shares to 40.5m.
summary: Neuberger Private Equity Partners Limited repurchased 24,848 Class A shares on 22 July 2026 under shareholder authority granted 11 June 2026, with prices ranging from £14.32 to £14.64. All shares will be cancelled. Post-cancellation, outstanding Class A shares total 40,489,301, with an additional 3,150,408 held in treasury. This reduces equity base while maintaining capital efficiency for the closed-end investment company.
retail_take: Routine capital management action; modest buyback signals confidence in valuation but has minimal market impact given share count reduction of 0.06%.

company: BlackRock Income and Growth Investment Trust Plc
headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Income and Growth Investment Trust discloses voting rights and capital structure as at 23 July 2026.
summary: As at 23 July 2026, BlackRock Income and Growth Investment Trust had 18,544,568 ordinary shares of 1 pence each in issue (excluding treasury shares), each carrying one vote. The company held 10,081,532 ordinary shares in treasury, representing 35.22% of total issued capital including treasury shares. Shareholders must use 18,544,568 as the denominator for calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA rules.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of voting rights and capital structure; no material implications for shareholders or market direction.

headline: Director Declaration
brief: Admiral Group Chair Mike Rogers steps down from Experian plc board effective 22 July 2026.
summary: Mike Rogers, Chair of Admiral Group plc, has resigned from his position as Chair and Director of Experian plc effective 22 July 2026. This is a routine director declaration announcement disclosing changes to Rogers' external board positions. The announcement provides contact details for media and investor enquiries regarding Admiral Group.
retail_take: Routine governance disclosure with no material impact on Admiral Group. Rogers remains as Admiral Chair; this concerns only his separate Experian directorship. Likely a non-event for markets.

company: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust Plc
headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust updates voting rights denominator to 90.3m shares as at 23 July 2026.
summary: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc disclosed that as at 23 July 2026, its issued capital comprised 90,328,001 ordinary shares of £0.001 each, excluding 27,600,937 shares held in treasury. Shareholders must use the 90,328,001 figure (shares excluding treasury holdings) as the denominator for calculating notification thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules 5.6.1.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure for share capital notification purposes; no material implications for share price or investor positions. Non-event.
